name: designing-architecture
description: Designs software architecture and selects appropriate patterns for projects. Use when designing systems, choosing architecture patterns, structuring projects, making technical decisions, or when asked about microservices, monoliths, or architectural approaches.
capabilities: Recommends architecture patterns based on project size and team experience
Provides visual diagrams for Layered, Clean, Hexagonal, Event-Driven, and CQRS patterns
Offers directory structure templates for feature-based and layer-based organizations
Includes a trade-off analysis template for documenting architectural decisions
Presents a validation checklist to verify architecture selections
author: CloudAI-X
securitySummary: Pure markdown documentation skill containing only architectural guidance. The SKILL.md file contains ASCII diagrams using backticks, tables, checklists, and templates for architecture decisions. No executable code, no network operations, no filesystem access beyond reading its own files. All 52 static findings are false positives: backticks are markdown code block delimiters, not shell execution; 'cryptographic algorithm' flags triggered by architecture terminology misidentification; URL is standard metadata.
valueStatement: Choosing the right architecture pattern is critical for project success but requires evaluating many factors. This skill provides a systematic framework for selecting architecture patterns based on project size, team capabilities, and business requirements.
limitations: Does not generate code implementations for selected architectures
Does not estimate project timelines or resource requirements
Does not integrate with CI/CD pipelines or deployment systems

useCases.title: Evaluate pattern trade-offs
useCases.description: Compare architecture patterns using the trade-off analysis template to document decisions

useCases.title: Structure new projects
useCases.description: Select appropriate directory structures based on project size and feature organization

useCases.title: Validate architectural choices
useCases.description: Use validation checklists to ensure architecture matches team capabilities

promptTemplates.title: Quick pattern selection
promptTemplates.scenario: Choosing architecture for a new project
promptTemplates.prompt: What architecture pattern should I use for a [size] project with [team_size] developers working on [project_type]?

promptTemplates.title: Compare approaches
promptTemplates.prompt: Compare monolith and microservices architecture for a [scale] application. Consider team size of [n] and requirements for [requirements].

promptTemplates.title: Create directory structure
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a feature-based directory structure for a [tech_stack] application with [n] main features including users, orders, and payments.

promptTemplates.title: Document architecture decision
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me document the decision to use [pattern] over [alternative] for a [project_description]. Include trade-offs for complexity, scalability, and team familiarity.

bestPractices: Start with the simplest architecture that meets current requirements and evolve as complexity grows
Match architecture complexity to team experience and skills to ensure maintainability
Document architectural decisions including trade-offs and rationale for future reference
antiPatterns: Over-engineering early by choosing microservices when a monolith would suffice
Ignoring team capabilities when selecting architecture patterns
Failing to validate architecture selections against project requirements

faq.question: Which tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for architectural guidance.

faq.question: What project sizes are covered?
faq.answer: Small (<10K LOC) uses MVC, medium (10K-100K) uses Clean Architecture, large (>100K) uses Modular Monolith or Microservices.

faq.question: Can this skill integrate with existing projects?
faq.answer: This is a guidance-only skill. It provides patterns and templates but does not modify project files.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill contains no executable code. It only provides architectural guidance through prompts.

faq.question: Why was my architecture recommendation rejected?
faq.answer: Validation checks may fail if the pattern does not match project size, team skills, or scalability requirements.

faq.question: How does this compare to hiring an architect?
faq.answer: This skill provides structured guidance but cannot replace domain expertise or complex stakeholder negotiations.
